articles.title: Literate Systems Lab

articles.bodyPreview:
Multiple Org files should feel like a blog index first, then become records,
agenda groups, and AI handoff context once selected.
articles.title: Agenda Lab Notes

articles.bodyPreview:
The public demo should prove that a personal site can read serious Org, not only
three TODO rows.  This source intentionally mixes prose, tables, links, drawers,
timestamps, source blocks, lists, footnotes, and planning metadata.


The key rule is simple: Type
articles.title: Org Syntax Atlas

articles.bodyPreview:
Research writing is a different stress test from agenda writing.  It uses links,
citations-like references, quoted excerpts, tables, and memory records.


#+BEGIN_QUOTE
The frontend should help a reader move from source evidence to a next action
without preten
articles.title: Research Notebook

articles.bodyPreview:
Zen mode is not the Blog section itself.  Blog is the library; Zen mode is the
reading posture for one selected article.


The article surface therefore needs enough typography to carry real writing:
paragraph rhythm, headings, footnotes, quotes, lists, and oc
articles.title: Zen Essay Notebook

articles.bodyPreview:
The first milestone is a browser surface that can display blog notes, records,
agenda-like planning data, links, properties, and attachment evidence from the
same Org source.
articles.title: Org Zhixing Demo
